response.justification: James Smith is a capable generalist engineer with strong TypeScript and Node.js credentials and directly relevant MCP/agentic workflow work in his current role at re:cinq, which is a genuine positive signal. However, the resume fails to meet several non-negotiable requirements: Python is entirely absent from both the skills section and every work history entry despite being a primary language for the role; there is no evidence of vector database, RAG architecture, or LLM evaluation pipeline experience; and his GenAI-specific production work appears to have started only in April 2025, far short of the 8-year minimum. The position also demands customer-facing technical leadership and enterprise consulting delivery, neither of which appears in the resume.
strengths: Hands-on MCP (Model Context Protocol) server and agentic workflow experience at re:cinq maps directly onto a listed key technology
Proven AWS cloud experience (AWS CDK at Optilyz) and deep TypeScript expertise satisfy two core required technologies
High-throughput data pipeline background at Dunnhumby (millions of requests/second, real-time metadata) demonstrates systems-scale engineering
concerns: Python is completely absent from the resume — not in the skills list, not in any job description, not in any project — despite being a primary required language
No mention of vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM performance metrics, or any modern GenAI evaluation tooling anywhere in the resume
Substantive AI/GenAI experience is limited to the role started April 2025; the position requires 8+ years of production AI delivery, leaving a roughly 6-year gap

key_factors.factor: No Python or RAG stack experience
